data.title: Associated British Foods Share Buyback Update
data.description:
Associated British Foods (GB:ABF) has released an update.

Associated British Foods plc has announced the purchase and subsequent cancellation of 100,522 of its own ordinary shares on October 10, 2024, as part of its share repurchase program initiated on September 11, 2024. The shares were bought at an average price of GBp 2320.7965, with the highest price being GBp 2325.00 and the lowest GBp 2313.00. All transactions were conducted on the London Stock Exchange and other trading platforms in accordance with market rules.

For further insights into GB:ABF stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Tate & Lyle Completes Share Buyback Move
data.description:
Tate & Lyle (GB:TATE) has released an update.

Tate & Lyle PLC has successfully bought back 210,802 of its own shares on 10 October 2024, with the transaction involving a volume weighted average price of £6.92 per share. The repurchased shares are to be held in treasury, and after the transaction, Tate & Lyle’s treasury holds over 17 million shares, reducing the number of shares in circulation. This buyback is part of an ongoing programme initiated on 20 June 2024 to enhance shareholder value.

For further insights into GB:TATE stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: UIL Ltd Director Buys Company Shares
data.description:
UIL Ltd (GB:UTL) has released an update.

UIL Limited has publicly disclosed a transaction involving Peter Durhager, a Non-executive Director, who has purchased 11,891 ordinary shares at 107.00p each on the London Stock Exchange on 10 October 2024. This initial notification serves as a transparency measure for shareholders and potential investors.

For further insights into GB:UTL stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: NDK Acquires Majority Stake in Groupe Parot
data.description:
Groupe Parot SA (FR:ALPAR) has released an update.

Groupe Parot has announced the conclusion of a simplified public tender offer by NDK, resulting in NDK acquiring 87.59% of Groupe Parot’s share capital, with plans for a mandatory buyout of the remaining shares. The trading of Groupe Parot shares remains suspended until the completion of this buyout. Groupe Parot is a notable player in France’s automotive distribution network, with a history spanning 45 years and a significant turnover of 385.5 million euros as of the end of 2023.

For further insights into FR:ALPAR stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
data.author: TipRanks European Auto-Generated Newsdesk

data.title: Fidelity Japan Trust PLC Executes Share Buyback
data.description:
Fidelity Japan Trust PLC (GB:FJV) has released an update.

Fidelity Japan Trust PLC has announced the repurchase of 10,134 of its own shares on October 10, 2024, at an average price of 163.260 GBp per share. This buyback has resulted in a total of 18,725,304 shares held in Treasury, with the overall issued share capital now standing at 136,161,695. Post-transaction, the total voting rights in the company amount to 117,436,391, excluding shares held in Treasury, which have no voting rights.

For further insights into GB:FJV stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
data.author: TipRanks UK Auto-Generated Newsdesk

data.title: Rightmove Advances Share Buy-back Programme
data.description:
Rightmove (GB:RMV) has released an update.

Rightmove plc continued its share buy-back programme by purchasing 75,000 of its own shares at a volume weighted average price of 620.017p, with the highest and lowest transaction prices being 622.000p and 617.800p respectively. This latest buy-back represents 0.0095% of Rightmove’s pre-purchase voting rights, further reducing the total number of ordinary shares in issue to 788,271,772, excluding treasury shares. The aggregate number of shares bought back since the programme’s inception in December 2007 has reached 519,426,789.

For further insights into GB:RMV stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: ITV plc Advances Share Buyback Program
data.description:
ITV plc (GB:ITV) has released an update.

ITV plc has successfully bought back 1,736,259 of its own shares at prices ranging from 77.00 to 77.80 pence, with a view to cancel them, as part of the share buyback program initiated on March 7, 2024. Post-transaction, the company’s total voting rights stand at 3,912,438,464. Since the start of the buyback program, ITV has repurchased over 148 million shares at an average price of 76.58 pence each.

For further insights into GB:ITV stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Dr. Reddy’s Announces Subsidiary’s Liquidation
data.description:
Dr Reddy’s Laboratories (RDY) has released an update.

Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories Ltd. has announced the shareholder-approved voluntary liquidation of its non-material wholly-owned subsidiary, Imperial Owners and Land Possessions Private Limited, which will not have a significant impact on the company’s consolidated financials. The decision follows the subsidiary’s surrender of its NBFC registration with the RBI earlier in the year, aligning with regulatory compliance and operational streamlining. Details of the liquidation process are contingent on regulatory approvals and have been outlined in the company’s latest regulatory filing.

For further insights into RDY stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Newsdesk

data.title: Compagnie des Alpes Confirms CEO’s Continued Leadership
data.description:
Compagnie des Alpes (FR:CDA) has released an update.

Compagnie des Alpes has announced the continuation of Dominique Thillaud as CEO until the annual meeting in March 2025, with the intention of reappointing him for another four years. The company, with a diverse portfolio of mountain resorts, leisure parks, and hospitality services, is committed to ecological sustainability and aims for Net Zero Carbon by 2030. Key financial results will be released in October and December 2024.

For further insights into FR:CDA stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
data.author: TipRanks European Auto-Generated Newsdesk

data.title: THG PLC Displays Resilience in Q3 Financials
data.description:
THG (GB:THG) has released an update.

THG PLC reports a slight dip in continuing Q3 revenue, but shows positive performance in its Beauty and Ingenuity sectors, with Beauty achieving strong growth and Nutrition trends improving. The company is on track to meet its full-year performance within consensus range and is progressing towards a demerger of THG Ingenuity, aiming for inclusion in the FTSE UK Index by March 2025.

For further insights into GB:THG stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
